title: Marisa Pavan

infobox.value: Maria Luisa Pierangeli  (1932-06-19)19 June 1932  [Cagliari](/wiki/Cagliari "Cagliari"), [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy")

(19 June 1932 – 6 December 2023), known professionally as **Marisa Pavan**, was an Italian and French actress who first became known as the twin sister of film star [Pier Angeli](/wiki/Pier_Angeli "Pier Angeli") (Anna Maria Pierangeli) before achieving success in her screen career.[[1]](#cite_note-Allen-1) She received an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") nomination and won a [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") for her performance in the 1955 film *[The Rose Tattoo](/wiki/The_Rose_Tattoo_(film) "The Rose Tattoo (film)")*.

Smith, Harrison (8 December 2023). ["Marisa Pavan, Oscar-nominated actress in 'The Rose Tattoo,' dies at 91"](https://www.washingtonpost.com/obituaries/2023/12/08/marisa-pavan-dead/). *The Washington Post*.

Retrieved 25 March 2018 – via [Newspapers.com](/wiki/Newspapers.com "Newspapers.com"). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian and French actress (1932–2023)

| Marisa Pavan | |
| --- | --- |
| Pavan in 1965 | |
| Born | Maria Luisa Pierangeli  (1932-06-19)19 June 1932  [Cagliari](/wiki/Cagliari "Cagliari"), [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |
| Died | 6 December 2023(2023-12-06) (aged 91)  [Gassin](/wiki/Gassin "Gassin"), France |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 1952–1992 |
| Spouse | [Jean-Pierre Aumont](/wiki/Jean-Pierre_Aumont "Jean-Pierre Aumont")  ​  ​ (m. 1956; died 2001)​ |
| Children | 2 |
| Relatives | [Pier Angeli](/wiki/Pier_Angeli "Pier Angeli") (sister) |

**Maria Luisa Pierangeli** (19 June 1932 – 6 December 2023), known professionally as **Marisa Pavan**, was an Italian and French actress who first became known as the twin sister of film star [Pier Angeli](/wiki/Pier_Angeli "Pier Angeli") (Anna Maria Pierangeli) before achieving success in her screen career.[[1]](#cite_note-Allen-1) She received an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") nomination and won a [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") for her performance in the 1955 film *[The Rose Tattoo](/wiki/The_Rose_Tattoo_(film) "The Rose Tattoo (film)")*.

## Early life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marisa_Pavan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career")]

Pavan and her [fraternal twin](/wiki/Fraternal_twin "Fraternal twin") sister, [Anna Maria](/wiki/Pier_Angeli "Pier Angeli"), were born in 1932 in [Cagliari](/wiki/Cagliari "Cagliari"), [Sardinia](/wiki/Sardinia "Sardinia"), the children of Enrichetta ([née](/wiki/Birth_name#Maiden_and_married_names "Birth name") Romiti) and Luigi Pierangeli, a construction engineer, both from [Pesaro](/wiki/Pesaro "Pesaro"), [Marche](/wiki/Marche "Marche").[[1]](#cite_note-Allen-1) The two girls also had a younger sister, Patrizia Pierangeli, who became an actress as well. Her stage name, Pavan, was the surname of a Jewish general in the [Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") she and her family hid during the [Nazi occupation of Rome](/wiki/Operation_Achse "Operation Achse") in [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Pavan had no dramatic training when she signed a Hollywood contract with [Paramount](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures") at age 19.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Although her screen debut was in 1952 in *[What Price Glory](/wiki/What_Price_Glory_(1952_film) "What Price Glory (1952 film)")*,[[4]](#cite_note-dr-4) Pavan's breakthrough role came three years later, when she was cast as [Anna Magnani](/wiki/Anna_Magnani "Anna Magnani")'s daughter in *[The Rose Tattoo](/wiki/The_Rose_Tattoo_(film) "The Rose Tattoo (film)")*. That part was initially assigned to her twin sister, but by the time production began, Angeli was unavailable for the role.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Pavan's performance earned an Oscar nomination for [Best Supporting Actress](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actress "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress").[[5]](#cite_note-5) She also won the Golden Globe Award for ([Best Supporting Actress](/wiki/13th_Golden_Globe_Awards#Best_performance_by_an_actress_in_a_supporting_role_in_a_motion_picture "13th Golden Globe Awards")) for her performance in the film.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Pavan co-starred in films such as *[Diane](/wiki/Diane_(1956_film) "Diane (1956 film)")* (1956), *[The Man in the Gray Flannel Suit](/wiki/The_Man_in_the_Gray_Flannel_Suit "The Man in the Gray Flannel Suit")* (1956), *[The Midnight Story](/wiki/The_Midnight_Story "The Midnight Story")* (1957) and *[John Paul Jones](/wiki/John_Paul_Jones_(film) "John Paul Jones (film)")* (1959). She also played [Abishag](/wiki/Abishag "Abishag") in [King Vidor](/wiki/King_Vidor "King Vidor")'s biblical epic *[Solomon and Sheba](/wiki/Solomon_and_Sheba "Solomon and Sheba")* (1959). Her later films included *[A Slightly Pregnant Man](/wiki/A_Slightly_Pregnant_Man "A Slightly Pregnant Man")* (1973), *[Antoine and Sebastian](/wiki/Antoine_and_Sebastian "Antoine and Sebastian")* (1974) and the television miniseries *[The Moneychangers](/wiki/The_Moneychangers "The Moneychangers")* (1976). In 1985, she played [Chantal Dubujak](/wiki/List_of_Ryan%27s_Hope_characters "List of Ryan's Hope characters") on *[Ryan's Hope](/wiki/Ryan%27s_Hope "Ryan's Hope")*.

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marisa_Pavan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

On 27 March 1956, Pavan married French actor [Jean-Pierre Aumont](/wiki/Jean-Pierre_Aumont "Jean-Pierre Aumont") in [Santa Barbara, California](/wiki/Santa_Barbara,_California "Santa Barbara, California"), and the couple remained together until Aumont's death in 2001.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) They had two sons.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Pavan died at her home in [Gassin](/wiki/Gassin "Gassin"), [France](/wiki/France "France") on 6 December 2023, aged 91.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Selected filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marisa_Pavan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Selected filmography")]

* *[What Price Glory](/wiki/What_Price_Glory_(1952_film) "What Price Glory (1952 film)")* (1952)
* *[I Chose Love](/wiki/I_Chose_Love "I Chose Love")* (1953)
* *[Down Three Dark Streets](/wiki/Down_Three_Dark_Streets "Down Three Dark Streets")* (1954)
* *[Drum Beat](/wiki/Drum_Beat "Drum Beat")* (1954)
* *[The Rose Tattoo](/wiki/The_Rose_Tattoo_(film) "The Rose Tattoo (film)")* (1955)
* *[Alfred Hitchcock Presents](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock_Presents "Alfred Hitchcock Presents")* (1956) (Season 1 Episode 16: "You Got to Have Luck") as Mary Schaffner
* *[Diane](/wiki/Diane_(1956_film) "Diane (1956 film)")* (1956)
* *[The Man in the Gray Flannel Suit](/wiki/The_Man_in_the_Gray_Flannel_Suit "The Man in the Gray Flannel Suit")* (1956)
* *[The Midnight Story](/wiki/The_Midnight_Story "The Midnight Story")* (1957)
* *[John Paul Jones](/wiki/John_Paul_Jones_(film) "John Paul Jones (film)")* (1959)
* *[Solomon and Sheba](/wiki/Solomon_and_Sheba "Solomon and Sheba")* (1959)
* *Shangri-La* (TV movie, 1960)
* *[Naked City](/wiki/Naked_City_(TV_series) "Naked City (TV series)")* – "Requiem for a Sunday Afternoon" as Josephine (1961)
* *[Combat](/wiki/Combat!_(TV_series) "Combat! (TV series)")* – Season 2 Episode 12 "Ambush" as Marie Marchand (1963)
* *[The F.B.I.](/wiki/The_F.B.I._(TV_series) "The F.B.I. (TV series)")* – Season 1 Episode 9 "The Exiles" as Maria Blanca (1965)
* *The Diary of Anne Frank* (TV movie, 1967)
* *Cutter's Trail* (TV movie) (1970)
* *[A Slightly Pregnant Man](/wiki/A_Slightly_Pregnant_Man "A Slightly Pregnant Man")* (1973)
* *[Antoine and Sebastian](/wiki/Antoine_and_Sebastian "Antoine and Sebastian")* (1974)
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (TV, 1977) "East Wind, Ill Wind" – Madame Sandanarik
* *[The Trial of Lee Harvey Oswald](/wiki/The_Trial_of_Lee_Harvey_Oswald_(1977_film) "The Trial of Lee Harvey Oswald (1977 film)")* (TV movie, 1977)
* *[Wonder Woman](/wiki/Wonder_Woman_(TV_series) "Wonder Woman (TV series)")* "Formula 407" (TV, 1977)
